headline: Latest News In AI Chips - AI Market Booms With Projections of Exponential Growth
summary: The global AI chip market is poised for significant expansion with projections indicating a leap from USD 102.89 billion in 2025 to an impressive USD 1354.35 billion by 2035, reflecting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 29.4% during the forecast period. This remarkable growth is largely attributed to the increasing demand for advanced computing capabilities driven by the adoption of AI across sectors such as data centers, cloud environments, and consumer applications. Key technologies,...

headline: Why Q2 earnings will be so important, according to Liz Ann Sonders
summary: US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) continue to rally in Wednesday's session — the first trading day of 2026's second quarter — coming off the Dow's meteoric 1,100-point gain on Tuesday.Charles Schwab chief investment strategist Liz Ann Sonders sits down with Brooke DiPalma to share how she is assessing all the market uncertainties and how it lines up with her long-term investing strategy.

content.title: Why Q2 earnings will be so important, according to Liz Ann Sonders
content.description:
US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) continue to rally in Wednesday's session — the first trading day of 2026's second quarter — coming off the Dow's meteoric 1,100-point gain on Tuesday.

Charles Schwab chief investment strategist Liz Ann Sonders sits down with Brooke DiPalma to share how she is assessing all the market uncertainties and how it lines up with her long-term investing strategy.
content.summary: US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) continue to rally in Wednesday's session — the first trading day of 2026's second quarter — coming off the Dow's meteoric 1,100-point gain on Tuesday.Charles Schwab chief investment strategist Liz Ann Sonders sits down with Brooke DiPalma to share how she is assessing all the market uncertainties and how it lines up with her long-term investing strategy.

headline: Micron Soars 9%: 3 Reasons the Memory Supercycle Is Reasserting Itself After a Rough Week
summary: Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) shares are up 9% in Wednesday morning trading, climbing from an opening price of $337.84 to around $370. The bounce follows a brutal stretch in which MU shares had declined 14.55% over the past week, dragged lower by fears surrounding Google’s TurboQuant memory-compression algorithm. headline: Stocks- Our Best Defense Remains a Strong Offense
summary: The fog of war persists, while global economic issues are emerging now as the primary reason to implement a cease-fire.
